rationale: The page contains detailed statistical data on health indicators (U5MR, Sex Ratio, TFR, Life Expectancy, Institutional Deliveries) and an appendix detailing key sports regulatory bodies in India (MYAS, SAI, IOA). This information is highly relevant for governance and social issues topics.
cleanedText:
### Health Indicators
**Under-five Mortality Rate (U5MR)**
* DECLINED from 45 per 1000 live births in 2014 to 31 per 1000 live births in 2021. [SDG target of U5MR (<=25 by 2030)]
* States: Lowest: Kerala; Highest: Madhya Pradesh
* As per Children in India 2025 Report, U5MR Declined from 30 (2022) to 29 (2023).
**Sex Ratio at Birth (Number of females per 1000 males at birth)**
* INCREASED from 907 in 2018-20 to 913 in 2019-21.
* States: Highest: Kerala (962); Lowest: Uttarakhand (852)
* Rural (2019-21): 912; Urban (2019-21): 918
* As per Civil Registration System Report, 2023 by RGI, it is Highest in Arunachal Pradesh (1,085) and Lowest in Jharkhand (899)
**Total Fertility Rate (TFR) (Average number of children a woman would have at end of their reproductive period)**
* Total Fertility Rate (TFR) = 2.0
* DECLINED during 2016-2021 at National level.
* States: Highest: Bihar (3.0); Lowest: Delhi and West Bengal (1.4)
* Replacement level TFR, viz. 2.1, has been attained at National level along with 16 States/UTs.
* Rural: 2.2; Urban: 1.6
**Life-expectancy at birth**
* 69.8 years (for period 2017-21), registering a decline of 0.2 years from 2016-20.
* For males has been estimated at 68.2 years and that for females, at 71.6 years.
**Institutional Deliveries**
* In 2021, ~91.1% of deliveries was institutional (Government and private hospitals).
* Urban areas: 95.5%; Rural areas: 89.7%
### 9.5. APPENDIX 5: KEY SPORTS REGULATORY BODIES
**Ministry of Youth and Sports (MYAS)**
* Functioned under overall guidance of Minister of State (Independent Charge) for Youth Affairs & Sports.
* In 2008, two separate Departments, namely, Department of Youth Affairs and Department of Sports, were created under Ministry.
* Each department under the charge of a Secretary to Government of India.
* Responsible for formulating national sports policy, funding and implementing development programmes.
**Sports Authority of India (SAI)**
* Set up in 1984 as a Society registered under Societies Act, 1860 to carry forward the legacy of 9th Asian Games held in New Delhi in 1982 under Department of Sports.
* Fully funded by Government of India and is under Administrative control of MYAS.
* Lays down policies and guidelines for sports delivery systems.
**Indian Olympic Association (IOA)**
* Established in 1927 with Sir Dorabji Tata and Dr. A.G. Noehren as Founding President and Secretary General respectively.
* Governed by a 32-member Executive Council. Election for Executive Council is held once in every 4 years.
* Recognised by MYAS and registered as a Non-Profit Organisation under Societies Registration Act, 1860.
* Governing body for Olympic Movement and Commonwealth Games in India.
* Affiliated member of International Olympic Committee (IOC), Commonwealth Games Federation (CGF), Olympic Council of Asia (OCA) and Association of National Olympic Committees (ANOC).
* Responsible for India’s participation in Olympic Games and other international multi-sports events.
